items.title: Loss surface axes
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  params["parameters theta"] --> surface["loss L of theta"]
  data["training data"] --> surface
  surface --> height["vertical: loss value"]
  params --> axes["horizontal: each dimension of theta"]
items.caption: Training data and the loss definition together determine the height L(θ) at every parameter point θ.

items.title: Optimizer path on a contour landscape
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  start["start theta"] -->|"follow negative gradient"| step1["lower loss"]
  step1 --> step2["lower loss"]
  step2 --> minimum["local or global minimum"]
items.caption: Gradient-based optimization traces a path across the loss surface toward a minimum, not a jump straight to it.

items.title: Local minimum vs saddle
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  crit["point where gradient is zero"] --> fork{"curvature in all directions?"}
  fork -->|"all up"| localmin["local minimum"]
  fork -->|"some up some down"| saddle["saddle point"]
  fork -->|"all down"| localmax["local maximum"]
items.caption: Zero gradient alone does not imply a minimum; saddle points are common in high-dimensional non-convex landscapes.
